Sebastian Raschka, PhD Jan 31

Ch 6 on reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards is now available. This chapter walks through GRPO from scratch, including a minimal but complete training loop with all the fun stuff: reward normalization, advantage estimation, etc.

And no worries, I'd say it is less complicated than it sounds. It is probably my favorite chapter so far. (And with this, the first 363 pages of the book are now complete.)

On a little tangent: you probably saw the clawdbot / OpenClaw developments in recent days. Essentially, it's a next-token predicting (reasoning) model combined with some looping, tool-use, orchestration, and recursion. And it's fascinating how these ingredients add up to something that is actually quite capable and quite autonomous (PS: I don't think clawdbot is taking over the world; but it's an interesting case study of how far the LLM/reasoning model can go if it is packaged up in a certain way.)

More broadly, what I am trying to say is that building methods from scratch helps us understand and appreciate the tools we rely on every day. For instance, RL with verifiable rewards is probably THE core ingredient of reasoning models and agent-style setups these days.

I

am currently working on the follow-up chapter with additional RLVR runs, more detailed metrics and analyses of training dynamics, and extensions like policy ratio clipping and KL regularization. (These were intentionally left out in chapter 6 to keep the initial GRPO introduction simple, readable, and closer in spirit to REINFORCE.)

I also have a long list of around 15 GRPO-related algorithmic tweaks queued up to run and analyze next. Fun times.

Here is the early-access link: https://mng.bz/Nwr7

Happy weekend!

Commenter One

This looks amazing! Can't wait to read through the GRPO chapter. The from-scratch approach really helps with understanding.

Commenter Two

Great work Sebastian. The training dynamics analysis section is exactly what I was hoping for.
